message:
beads: close additional conformance beads

Closed:
- bd-1e1b: Rust QuickJS runtime validation (60 extensions load)
- bd-1v10: Phase 0 Environment Setup (both runtimes working)

Conformance infrastructure now complete for official extensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -51,7 +51,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dd3","title":"Interactive: render /hotkeys from active keybindings","description":"# Goal
Replace the static `/hotkeys` output with a dynamically generated view from the active keybindings.

# Required Behavior
- `/hotkeys` should list shortcuts grouped by category, mirroring the legacy docs (Cursor Movement, Deletion, Text Input, Application, Session, Models & Thinking, Display, Message Queue, Selection).
- If user overrides are loaded, `/hotkeys` must reflect them.

# Nice-to-have (but keep scope tight)
- Show both default and overridden keys (e.g., dim default, bright override).
- Include a hint for where to edit: `~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json`.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/hotkeys` is generated from action catalog + bindings.
- [ ] Output is stable and testable (snapshot).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"opus","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:55.925113647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:07.029739164Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:42.591862805Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-3qm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","depends_on_id":"bd-cru","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-1dd3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"/hotkeys dynamic rendering implementation complete (bd-1dd3).

**Changes to src/interactive.rs:**

1. **Added `format_hotkeys()` method:**
   - Iterates through all action categories using `ActionCategory::all()`
   - Groups actions by category with display headers
   - Shows key bindings from the active keybindings (supports user overrides)
   - Includes config file path for user reference
   - Skips empty categories (actions without bindings)

2. **Updated SlashCommand::Hotkeys handler:**
   - Replaced static string with dynamic `self.format_hotkeys()` call
   - Output now reflects actual active keybindings including user overrides

**Output format:**
```
Keyboard Shortcuts
==================

Config: ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json

## Cursor Movement

  up                   Move cursor up
  down                 Move cursor down
  left, ctrl+b         Move cursor left
  ...

## Application

  escape               Cancel / abort
  ctrl+c               Clear editor
  ...
```

**Test added (tests/tui_state.rs):**
- `tui_state_slash_hotkeys_shows_dynamic_keybindings` - verifies output contains bindings and descriptions

**Tests: All 51 TUI tests passing, 207+ total**
","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:18:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1djr","title":"Set coverage targets per extension type","description":"# Goal
Define explicit coverage targets so the final set spans all extension shapes and high‑value categories.

# Deliverables
- Minimum counts per type (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates, bundles).
- Category coverage (e.g., search, codegen, devops, data, infra, UI, analytics).
- Rationale tying targets to user value and runtime capabilities.

# Notes
Targets prevent popularity bias from dominating the selection.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:26.684401576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:03:17.160455837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:03:17.160388572Z","close_reason":"Defined Tier-0/Tier-1 coverage targets per extension shape and behavior buckets in EXTENSIONS.md (§1C.5)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1djr","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:26.690551132Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":333,"issue_id":"bd-1djr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: A ranked list can still miss key extension types.

Reasoning: Explicit coverage targets guarantee we validate the full breadth of extension shapes and user workflows.

Considerations: Targets should be achievable given available artifacts and runtime capabilities.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:53Z"},{"id":375,"issue_id":"bd-1djr","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"COVERAGE TARGETS: SCALE + QUOTAS

In addition to per-shape minimums, set an explicit overall size target:
- Tier-1 corpus size target: >= 200 extensions

Quotas (example framing)
- Ensure Tier-1 has enough of each high-value shape to exercise runtime thoroughly:
  - tool-only, command, event hooks, UI/RPC, provider registration
  - deps-heavy multi-file packages
  - exec/http/fs heavy extensions

Why this improves user outcomes
- Users care that “extensions people actually use” work. Size + stratified quotas reduce the chance we miss a major class of real-world behavior.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:10:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e0","title":"Implement extension discovery + install resolution","description":"Background:
- pi CLI must discover extensions from packages and CLI flags; this is core UX.

Steps:
- Extend ResourceLoader/PackageManager to resolve extension assets and manifests.
- Respect CLI flags (--extension/--no-extensions) and precedence rules (project > global).
- Produce structured diagnostics for missing/invalid/conflicting extensions.
- Ensure resolution is deterministic and cache-safe for repeatable tests.

Logging requirements:
- Diagnostics include source, resolution path, and reason codes.

Acceptance:
- Extensions can be installed, discovered, and enumerated from settings + CLI.
- Diagnostics are actionable and stable for unit/E2E assertions.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleCreek","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:23:58.695313308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:46:38.653018635Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:46:38.652954255Z","close_reason":"All unit+E2E tests green for extension discovery/resolution (cargo test --test package_manager/resource_loader/e2e_cli). CLI flags + precedence + diagnostics covered; ready to unblock dependents.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-3sf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e0","depends_on_id":"bd-gqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1e1b","title":"Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations","description":"# Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations

## Context
The Rust extension runtime uses:
- src/extensions_js.rs: JsExtensionLoadSpec, swc TypeScript compilation, QuickJS execution
- src/extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (pattern detection), load_all_extensions(), ExtensionManager
- src/extension_dispatcher.rs: hostcall dispatch

Extensions are loaded via JsExtensionLoadSpec -> swc transpiles TS to JS -> QuickJS executes -> snapshot_extensions() captures JsExtensionSnapshot (tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers).

## What To Do
1. Write a minimal Rust test that creates a JsExtensionLoadSpec for hello.ts
2. Load it through the extension pipeline
3. Capture the resulting JsExtensionSnapshot
4. Verify the snapshot contains expected registrations
5. Serialize the snapshot as JSON for comparison

## Key Files
- src/extensions_js.rs (JsExtensionLoadSpec, load flow)
- src/extensions.rs (CompatibilityScanner, ExtensionManager)
- tests/e2e_extension_registration.rs (existing test patterns)

## Acceptance Criteria
- hello.ts loads without panics
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures expected registrations
- Snapshot can be serialized to JSON","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:56.677449417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:00.761438136Z","close_reason":"Created tests/e2e_ts_extension_loading.rs with 13 tests validating the full TypeScript extension loading pipeline: swc transpilation → QuickJS execution → ExtensionManager registration capture → JSON serialization. Tests cover: minimal TS (type annotations), rich TS (interfaces, generics, type aliases, multiple registration types), const enums, type-only constructs, empty extensions, load spec metadata with/without package.json, multiple TS extensions, command/shortcut execution, and JSON snapshot round-trip. All 13 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e1b","depends_on_id":"bd-1v10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:56.692032037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1e1b","title":"Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations","description":"# Validate Rust QuickJS runtime can load extensions and capture registrations

## Context
The Rust extension runtime uses:

- src/extensions.rs: CompatibilityScanner (pattern detection), load_all_extensions(), ExtensionManager

Extensions are loaded via JsExtensionLoadSpec -> swc transpiles TS to JS -> QuickJS executes -> snapshot_extensions() captures JsExtensionSnapshot (tools, slash_commands, shortcuts, flags, event_hooks, providers).

## What To Do
1. Write a minimal Rust test that creates a JsExtensionLoadSpec for hello.ts
2. Load it through the extension pipeline
3. Capture the resulting JsExtensionSnapshot
4. Verify the snapshot contains expected registrations
5. Serialize the snapshot as JSON for comparison

## Key Files
- src/extensions_js.rs (JsExtensionLoadSpec, load flow)
- src/extensions.rs (CompatibilityScanner, ExtensionManager)
- tests/e2e_extension_registration.rs (existing test patterns)

## Acceptance Criteria
- hello.ts loads without panics
- JsExtensionSnapshot captures expected registrations
- Snapshot can be serialized to JSON","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:56.677449417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:35:55.801256207Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:35:55.801191697Z","close_reason":"Rust QuickJS runtime validated: hello.ts and all 60 official extensions load successfully. JsExtensionSnapshot captures registrations (tools, commands, flags, shortcuts, handlers). Differential conformance tests compare serialized JSON snapshots against TS oracle. All acceptance criteria met.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e1b","depends_on_id":"bd-1v10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:16:56.692032037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1elj","title":"Enable markdown feature in rich_rust for assistant response rendering","description":"Enable the markdown feature in rich_rust to properly render assistant responses with formatting.

## Current State
- rich_rust has features = ["full"] but markdown rendering not used
- Assistant responses with markdown (headers, bold, lists, code blocks) render as plain text
- glamour is used for markdown in interactive TUI but not for print mode

## Implementation
1. Ensure 'markdown' feature is enabled in Cargo.toml (check if included in 'full')
2. Use rich_rust::Markdown in print mode output (src/main.rs, src/tui.rs)
3. Ensure PiConsole has method to render markdown content
4. Test with various markdown constructs

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~100KB (pulldown-cmark dependency)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: markdown rendering produces expected ANSI output
- Manual test: pi -p 'Write a list of 3 items' shows formatted list

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (verify feature)
- src/tui.rs (add markdown rendering method)
- src/main.rs (use for print mode)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.228230583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960168833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:29:47.960105465Z","close_reason":"Completed: print-mode assistant output renders Markdown via rich_rust::Markdown; unit tests added; gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1elj","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:33.234790707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1f5","title":"Extensions: QuickJS runtime + Pi event loop (no Node/Bun)","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires deterministic event loop semantics without Node/Bun.
- Assume **QuickJS has no WebAssembly**: wasm-using JS bundles must use the PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry) or Tier A WASM components.

Steps:
- Embed QuickJS via safe Rust bindings and expose a minimal Pi event loop.
- Implement microtask + promise ordering and timer scheduling per bd-123.
- Provide a hostcall bridge: JS promises resolve/reject only via the connector dispatcher (timeouts/cancel/policy errors correctly mapped).
- Load/inject required compatibility shims (Node core modules + globals) via the extc pipeline + module loader contract.
- Emit structured logs for JS task scheduling, hostcalls, policy decisions, and wasm-bridge activity.

Acceptance (hard):
- JS-tier runtime can execute the **full pinned sample set** where applicable: **16/16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run with no manual source edits**, using shims/rewrites/bridge as needed.
- Deterministic ordering is testable and reproducible under harness control.

Notes:
- Node core module shims are tracked in bd-3d0 (non-child_process) and bd-2sr (child_process).
- WebAssembly-in-JS is tracked in bd-1ry.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
[ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
[ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:24:24.611922334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:34:08.350173958Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-8mm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1f5","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting work on bd-1f5. Plan: review existing extension runtime scaffolding (src/extensions.rs, src/interactive.rs, src/resources.rs), identify integration points for a QuickJS host/event loop, and draft initial design + module skeleton. If anyone is already working on QuickJS bindings, please flag conflicts.","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:54:15Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress: added QuickJS runtime scaffolding in src/extensions_js.rs (AsyncRuntime/AsyncContext creation, script eval helpers, job draining, and stub pi.* hostcalls). Added clippy allowance for non-Send futures. Tests: cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:06:57Z"},{"id":211,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"BlackOwl","text":"Picking up bd-1f5 build break: src/extensions_js.rs SWC/TS transpile code doesn’t compile against current swc_* crate APIs (resolver/strip/new_source_file/etc). Plan: fix API usage, remove any leftover debug prints in rpc tests, then run cargo fmt/check/clippy/test. FYI I saw Agent Mail file reservations on src/extensions_js.rs / tests/rpc_mode.rs (RainyStone, RusticBadger) and requested contact to coordinate.","created_at":"2026-02-04T05:31:45Z"},{"id":215,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"BlackOwl","text":"Progress: repo back to green. Fixed clippy/warnings in src/extensions_js.rs (resolver lifetime elision, match arm merge, redundant clone, Path-based tsx detection, allow too_many_lines for virtual module stubs, base64 encode uses u8::try_from). Cleaned unused imports in src/extensions.rs. Minor rpc.rs match arm tidy. Tests: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-04T06:02:24Z"},{"id":279,"issue_id":"bd-1f5","author":"ChartreusePond","text":"Fix: make streamSimple cancel-on-drop reliable. In src/extensions.rs, provider_stream_simple_cancel_best_effort() now try_sends cancel, and if the bounded command channel is full it spawns a small current-thread runtime to send().await so cancellation is not silently dropped. Verified providers::tests::extension_stream_simple_provider_drop_cancels_js_stream passes; full gates: fmt/check/clippy/test.","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:34:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fc4","title":"Define performance budgets + metrics","description":"# Goal
Set explicit performance and reliability budgets for extension execution.

# Deliverables
- Budget targets (startup ms, tool invocation latency, memory).
- Measurement methodology and environment assumptions.
- Thresholds for pass/fail gating.

# Notes
Budgets must be realistic and enforceable in CI.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:25.369117348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:39.671212395Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fc4","depends_on_id":"bd-205q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:32:29.869120320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":349,"issue_id":"bd-1fc4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: Performance goals must be explicit to be enforceable.

Reasoning: Budgets allow us to say what "fast" means for extension startup and execution.

Considerations: Specify environment and measurement method to keep results comparable.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:52:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1uj","title":"Unit tests: WASM hostcalls + sandbox boundaries","description":"Background:
- WASM hostcalls must enforce capabilities and sandboxing rigorously.

Steps:
- Unit tests for each hostcall (read/write/exec/http/env) with allow/deny cases.
- Ensure errors propagate with correct codes/messages.
- Validate resource cleanup and cancellation behavior.

Logging requirements:
- Tests assert hostcall logs include extension id, capability, and decision.

Acceptance:
- Hostcall behavior matches policy and is stable under error conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

 {"id":"bd-1uk","title":"Connector: process/env/path/crypto/time minimal APIs","description":"# Goal
Fill remaining minimal OS surface in PiJS without pulling in Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `pi.env.get` (whitelisted keys only).
- `pi.process.cwd`, `pi.process.args` (read-only).
- `pi.path` helpers (join, basename, normalize).
- `pi.crypto` subset (hashing, random bytes) backed by Rust.
- `pi.time` (monotonic now, sleep via timers).
- Structured logs for env access and crypto ops (non-secret metadata only).

# Pinned-sample requirements (must support)
The Node shims (bd-3d0) need these behaviors to be available under capability enforcement:
- A **virtual HOME** consistent with `os.homedir()`.
- A safe `process.env` view that includes:
  - `HOME`
  - any explicitly allowed extension-specific keys (e.g. `PI_IMAGE_SAVE_MODE`, `PI_IMAGE_SAVE_DIR`) while default-denying everything else.

# Security Invariants
- No ability to read arbitrary env vars.
- Randomness and hashing are constant-time and auditable.

# Tests
- Unit tests for env allowlist enforcement.
- Deterministic hashing for test seeds (if provided).
- E2E security suite validates env denial.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftRiver","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:47.943408211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:10.775885156Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:49:44.118677406Z","close_reason":"Completed (PiJS env/process/path/crypto/time surface + allowlist tests; build/clippy/fmt/test green)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uk","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uk","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":225,"issue_id":"bd-1uk","author":"VioletSpring","text":"Implemented bd-1uk hardening in src/extensions_js.rs: env allowlist enforced in __pi_env_get_native (HOME/OS/OSTYPE/PI_*), froze pi.process + args and globalThis.process + argv/envProxy, updated node:os.homedir() to use HOME, strengthened unit tests in src/extensions_js.rs. Cargo test passes, but clippy is currently blocked by unrelated src/extensions.rs wasm_host bindgen compile errors + reserved interactive/session clippy issues.","created_at":"2026-02-04T08:32:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy","title":"Workstream: Rust extension runtime compatibility complete","description":"Purpose:
- Implement extension runtime support in pi_agent_rust so extensions run *as-is* with correct protocol, capabilities, and event wiring.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Extension manifest loader + compatibility scanner.
- WASM host + WIT bindings; JS compatibility pipeline.
- Runtime wiring for tool_call, slash_command, event_hooks.
- Configurable policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) with audit logging.

Definition of done:
- The runtime can load sample extensions and expose their tools/commands.
- Capability policy is enforced consistently and logged.
- No modifications to extension source are required (as-is).

Dependencies:
- Must align with extension protocol schema + WIT spec.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] All child/dependent beads include unit tests + E2E scripts with JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9 (or N/A with rationale) and are closed
- [ ] Unit/integration tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this feature area
- [ ] E2E scripts validate user-facing workflows; logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Performance/UX budgets or evidence updated where applicable; regressions are detectable
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

-{"id":"bd-1v10","title":"Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup","description":"# Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup

## Purpose
Before we can do ANY differential testing, we need BOTH runtimes working and verified independently. This phase ensures:
1. The pi-mono TypeScript runtime can load and run extensions via Bun
2. The Rust QuickJS runtime can load and run extensions
3. A deterministic test environment exists (no randomness, fixed timestamps)

## Why This Is Phase 0
Everything else depends on this. If either runtime cannot load extensions, differential testing is impossible. We discovered Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun. The pi-mono source lives at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/. We need to verify it builds and its extension loader works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pi-mono extension loader.ts can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Rust QuickJS runtime can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Both outputs can be captured as structured JSON
- Deterministic mode eliminates timestamps, random values, cwd-dependent paths","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.671337047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:14.284226664Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v10","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.724213001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":309,"issue_id":"bd-1v10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"KEY DISCOVERY: Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun, which also provides a node shim. pi-mono uses jiti for extension loading (not native Bun module resolution). The virtualModules mechanism in loader.ts maps package names to bundled versions (@sinclair/typebox, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, etc.). In development mode it uses aliases instead. For our harness, we need to run in development mode (not Bun binary mode) so that jiti resolves imports via filesystem.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:14Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1v10","title":"Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup","description":"# Phase 0: Environment and Runtime Setup

## Purpose
Before we can do ANY differential testing, we need BOTH runtimes working and verified independently. This phase ensures:
1. The pi-mono TypeScript runtime can load and run extensions via Bun
2. The Rust QuickJS runtime can load and run extensions
3. A deterministic test environment exists (no randomness, fixed timestamps)

## Why This Is Phase 0
Everything else depends on this. If either runtime cannot load extensions, differential testing is impossible. We discovered Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun. The pi-mono source lives at legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/. We need to verify it builds and its extension loader works.

## Acceptance Criteria
- pi-mono extension loader.ts can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Rust QuickJS runtime can load the hello.ts extension and produce output
- Both outputs can be captured as structured JSON
- Deterministic mode eliminates timestamps, random values, cwd-dependent paths","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.671337047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:36:15.918993495Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:36:15.918905561Z","close_reason":"Phase 0 complete: pi-mono loads extensions via Bun/jiti (tests/ext_conformance/ts_oracle/load_extension.ts), Rust QuickJS runtime loads extensions (tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs), both produce structured JSON, all 60 official extensions validated.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v10","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:14:07.724213001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":309,"issue_id":"bd-1v10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"KEY DISCOVERY: Bun 1.3.8 is available at /home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun, which also provides a node shim. pi-mono uses jiti for extension loading (not native Bun module resolution). The virtualModules mechanism in loader.ts maps package names to bundled versions (@sinclair/typebox, @mariozechner/pi-agent-core, etc.). In development mode it uses aliases instead. For our harness, we need to run in development mode (not Bun binary mode) so that jiti resolves imports via filesystem.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ve4","title":"CI: add cross-platform build/test matrix (linux/macOS/windows)","description":"# Goal
Increase release confidence by running CI on all target platforms (at minimum build + unit tests), and document any platform-specific exclusions.

# Background
The current CI workflow only runs on `ubuntu-latest`. That’s good for fast iteration but insufficient for shipping binaries.

# Scope
- Add a GitHub Actions matrix over:
  - `ubuntu-latest`
  - `macos-latest`
  - `windows-latest`
- For each OS:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test --all-targets`

# Platform Considerations
- System deps:
  - `fd` and `rg` are required/optional for tool tests; install equivalents per OS.
- Path dependencies:
  - CI currently clones sibling repos (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust/sqlmodel_rust). Ensure the same approach works on macOS/Windows.
- VCR playback mode:
  - Keep `VCR_MODE=playback` for determinism.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ci.yml` runs on Linux/macOS/Windows.
- [ ] Any OS-specific skips are explicit and justified (no silent test omissions).
- [ ] Failures include enough logs to debug platform-specific issues.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:48.739486913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:27:15.998454113Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:29:34.304318188Z","close_reason":"Cross-platform CI matrix in .github/workflows/ci.yml (commit 292102c)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ve4","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vmz","title":"Acquire npm artifacts (tarballs)","description":"# Goal
Download and vendor the selected npm‑hosted extensions as immutable tarballs.

- One folder per extension under tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/.

- Notes for any post‑download extraction needed by the harness (without modifying source).

# Notes
All artifacts must match upstream byte‑for‑byte.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:18.698014370Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:46.521353392Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vmz","depends_on_id":"bd-1pqf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:12.662924321Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1vmz","depends_on_id":"bd-2oal","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:18.712656710Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1vmz","depends_on_id":"bd-3vb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:04.560232676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":338,"issue_id":"bd-1vmz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: npm tarballs are the canonical distribution for many extensions.

Reasoning: Vendoring tarballs preserves upstream integrity and avoids local rebuild variability.

Considerations: Record dist.integrity and tarball URL for later verification.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:50:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vo","title":"Migrate OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate OpenAI provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/openai.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
OpenAI provider handles:
- Chat completions API
- Function calling (tools)
- SSE streaming with data: [DONE] termination
- 645 lines, 3 unit tests

## Implementation
Same pattern as Anthropic migration:
1. Replace reqwest::Client with crate::http::Client
2. Replace request building with asupersync RequestBuilder
3. Replace SSE streaming with SseStream adapter
4. Add Cx parameter to async functions

## OpenAI-Specific Considerations
1. **SSE Format**: Uses "data: [DONE]" as termination marker
2. **Tool Calls**: JSON in function_call field
3. **Streaming Deltas**: content delta in choices[0].delta.content
4. **Error Format**: Different error JSON structure from Anthropic

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)
- bd-37l (Anthropic migration first - establishes pattern)

## Testing
- 3 existing unit tests must pass
- Integration test with mock server

## Files
- src/providers/openai.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works
- [ ] Tool calls work","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"Connector: Filesystem minimal API (capability-scoped)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **tight, minimal filesystem connector** that supplies just enough for extensions without Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Minimal API: read, write, list, stat, mkdir, delete (if required).
- Path allowlist + canonicalization; no path traversal.
- Symlink policy: either disallow or resolve to allowlist roots.
- Explicit capability scopes (read/write per path prefix).
- Structured logs for each FS operation (path hash + scope).

# Security Invariants
- Every FS call checks capability scope before side effects.
- All paths are normalized and validated.

# Tests
- Unit tests for allowlist enforcement + symlink escapes.
- E2E security suite attempts traversal with log verification.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

## Context
The pi-mono repo includes a community/ directory with 58 community-contributed extensions. These are curated (accepted into the repo) so quality should be reasonable.

## Community Extensions by Author
- hjanuschka: clipboard, cost-tracker, flicker-corp, funny-working-message, handoff, loop, memory-mode, oracle, plan-mode, resistance, speedreading, status-widget, ultrathink, usage-bar (14)
- mitsuhiko: answer, control, cwd-history, files, loop, notify, review, todos, uv, whimsical (10)
- nicobailon: interactive-shell, interview-tool, mcp-adapter, powerline-footer, rewind-hook, subagents (6)
- tmustier: agent-guidance, arcade-mario-not, arcade-picman, arcade-ping, arcade-spice-invaders, arcade-tetris, code-actions, files-widget, ralph-wiggum, raw-paste, tab-status, usage-extension (12)
- qualisero: background-notify, compact-config, pi-agent-scip, safe-git, safe-rm, session-color, session-emoji (7)
- prateekmedia: checkpoint, lsp, permission, ralph-loop, repeat, token-rate (6)
- others: ferologics-notify, jyaunches-canvas, mrexodia-cost-dashboard, ogulcancelik-ghostty-theme-sync (4)

## Artifacts Location
tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 58 extensions attempted in differential runner
- 90%+ of pi-mono-loadable extensions pass
- All failures categorized: our_bug, extension_bug, missing_feature, platform_specific","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.653080335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.675567131Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.669335873Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-3ay7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.675544439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ru2","depends_on_id":"bd-7hgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:24:21.661154078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2s1z","title":"Set up CI pipeline for extension conformance (fast/full/weekly)","description":"# Set up CI pipeline for extension conformance (fast/full/weekly)

## What
Integrate the differential conformance runner into the project CI system. Three tiers of testing with different frequency and scope.

## Implementation
1. Add conformance feature flags to Cargo.toml
2. Create CI config (GitHub Actions or equivalent) with three jobs:
   - conformance-fast: runs on every PR push
   - conformance-full: runs nightly at 2 AM
   - conformance-weekly: runs Saturday night
3. Each job installs Bun, builds pi-mono deps, runs the conformance runner
4. Results uploaded as CI artifacts
5. Fast path failure blocks PR merge

## Dependencies
- Bun must be available in CI environment
- pi-mono deps must be installable
- All artifact extensions must be in the repo or downloaded during CI

## Acceptance Criteria
- conformance-fast runs in < 5 minutes
- conformance-full runs in < 30 minutes
- Results available as downloadable CI artifacts
- PR merge blocked on conformance-fast failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.958643960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.974767241Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s1z","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.974745430Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2s1z","depends_on_id":"bd-19rt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:52.968296587Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2skp","title":"Workstream: Message Queue Parity (steering vs follow-up)","description":"# Goal
Implement **legacy Pi message queue semantics** in interactive mode, including steering vs follow-up delivery, queue UI, and keybinding integration.

# Background (Legacy Spec)
Legacy pi-mono allows submitting messages while the agent is still working:

From `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/README.md` → “Message Queue”:
- **Enter** queues a *steering* message, delivered after current tool execution (interrupts remaining tools)
- **Alt+Enter** queues a *follow-up* message, delivered only after the agent finishes all work
- **Escape** aborts and restores queued messages to editor
- **Alt+Up** retrieves queued messages back to editor

Settings (legacy `docs/settings.md`):
- `steeringMode` and `followUpMode` can be `"one-at-a-time"` (default) or `"all"`.

# Current Rust State (Gap)
- `src/config.rs` already has `steering_mode` / `follow_up_mode`, but interactive does not implement the queue.
- Interactive key handling is hard-coded; Enter submits only when idle.

# Scope / Deliverables
## 1) Queue data model
- Represent queued items as:
  - kind: steering | follow_up
  - text
  - timestamp/sequence
- Store in PiApp state; ensure deterministic ordering.

## 2) Delivery points (core semantics)
- **Steering**: deliver after the current tool finishes (or next safe checkpoint) and cancel remaining tool iterations for the current user request.
- **Follow-up**: deliver only once the agent reaches an “idle” boundary (no more tools, assistant finished).

## 3) Modes: one-at-a-time vs all
- `one-at-a-time`:
  - deliver at most one queued message per boundary, wait for response before sending next.
- `all`:
  - flush all queued messages at the boundary (preserving order), then process.

## 4) Keybinding behavior
- Enter while busy → enqueue steering
- Alt+Enter while busy → enqueue follow-up
- Escape while busy → abort + restore all queued to editor
- Alt+Up → move queued back into editor for editing

(These should be expressed as actions and wired via the keybindings workstream.)

## 5) UI
- Render queued messages in a dedicated area (legacy has a pending messages container).
- Clear, minimal indicators:
  - show count by type
  - show the actual queued text (truncated) so the user knows what will be sent

# Testing
- Deterministic state machine tests for:
  - enqueue ordering
  - delivery boundaries
  - one-at-a-time vs all
  - abort + restore

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue behavior matches legacy semantics.
- [ ] Modes are configurable via settings.
- [ ] UI makes queued messages visible and understandable.

# Dependencies
- Depends on keybinding actions (`bd-3ip`) for correct shortcut routing.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-2sl9","title":"Proptest: SSE parser robustness (chunking + boundaries)","description":"## Goal
Property-test the SSE parser so arbitrary chunk boundaries and adversarial inputs don’t break parsing invariants.

## Why
SSE parsing is extremely sensitive to chunk boundaries. Real networks can split at any byte.

## Invariants
- Parsing is chunk-boundary invariant: splitting the same byte stream into random chunks yields identical parsed events.
- Parser never panics.
- Parser either produces valid events or a well-typed error (no silent corruption).
- For known-valid SSE inputs, the parser produces the expected event sequence.

## Acceptance
- New proptest module exists for `src/sse.rs`.
- CI runtime stays bounded.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
